expectations.evidence: Both analysis.md and architecture.md exist with substantial content.

expectations.text: A1: Identifies Microservices AND Service-Based as top candidates
expectations.evidence: Evaluates Microservices (4.30), Service-Based/Hybrid (4.00), and Modular Monolith (3.05). Recommends Microservices but notes Service-Based as 'Close second; viable if team prefers fewer, coarser services initially.'

expectations.text: A2: Shows ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles
expectations.evidence: Only evaluates 3 styles in the decision matrix: Microservices, Modular Monolith, Service-Based. Missing Layered, Microkernel, Event-Driven, Space-Based, and Service-Oriented from any comparison table.

expectations.text: A3: Acknowledges close scores and discusses qualifying context (team size, migration strategy, budget)
expectations.evidence: The decision matrix shows Microservices at 4.30 and Service-Based at 4.00 — these are relatively close, but the analysis does NOT explicitly acknowledge the closeness as a factor. There is no qualifying context discussion about team size, budget, or other factors that might tip the decision. The recommendation is presented as definitive.

expectations.text: A4: Discusses evolution path (gradual migration, strangler fig, or similar)
expectations.evidence: Detailed Strangler Fig migration strategy: Phase 1 (Catalog), Phase 2 (Checkout), Phase 3 (Fulfillment). architecture.md also mentions 'Strangler Fig: Catalog (Phase 1) -> Checkout (Phase 2) -> Fulfillment (Phase 3)'.

expectations.text: A5: architecture.md has structured selection section
expectations.evidence: architecture.md has 'Architecture Style Decision' with rationale, trade-offs, alternatives considered, and migration strategy. But there is NO evolution path beyond migration, and no structured format with explicit sections for partitioning type or cost category.
